DownloadsChangelogAbout
About Loom

Built for your machine.

Loom is a suite of offline-first desktop utilities for Windows, built on the belief that good software respects the machine it runs on — and the person running it.

The story

Loom started as a personal tool. The first app — Loom Macro — grew out of a simple frustration: every macro and automation tool I tried either required a subscription, phoned home, or hid your data behind an opaque sync engine.

The goal was straightforward: a powerful, visual macro editor that stores everything locally in a plain format you can read, back up, and delete whenever you want. Nothing more, nothing less.

That approach turned out to define something bigger. Every Loom app follows the same rule: your data lives on your machine. Not in our cloud. Not in our database. On your disk, in a folder you can open in Notepad if you feel like it.

The suite is growing slowly and deliberately. Each app ships when it's ready — not when a roadmap demands it. The design language is consistent, the offline guarantee is non-negotiable, and the source is open.

Principles

What Loom stands for.

01

Offline first, always.

Every Loom app runs without an internet connection. There are no sync servers, no cloud APIs, no telemetry endpoints. If your machine is offline, Loom still works.

02

Your data is yours.

Loom apps store data in open, documented formats. You can read the files in a text editor, import them into another tool, or delete them without asking us first.

03

No accounts. No friction.

You should be able to download an app and start using it immediately. No sign-up form. No email verification. No onboarding flow asking for your name.

04

Transparent about data.

Every Loom app exposes its data folder in-app. You can see exactly where your data lives, open it, inspect it, or delete it with one click — no support ticket required.

05

Open source.

The Loom suite is open source. You can read the code, verify the privacy guarantees, contribute improvements, or fork it. The openness is the guarantee.

◫
Data transparency

Open your data folder, anytime.

Every Loom app includes an "Open data folder" button in its settings panel. One click opens your file explorer to the exact directory where the app stores its data. The format is always documented plain text — JSON or similar.

App Data
Data location %APPDATA%\Loom\Macro
Format JSON, human-readable

Deleting your data is permanent and instant — no account to close, no retention period, no "are you sure?" forms beyond a single confirmation.

Open source

Read the code.

All Loom apps are open source under the MIT license. The source is the documentation.

View on GitHub →

LOOM

A suite of offline-first desktop utilities for Windows. Your data lives on your machine.
